title: Web Basics
description: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and browser fundamentals for new developers.

questions.question: What does HTML stand for?
questions.explanation: HTML is the markup language used to structure web pages.

questions.question: Which CSS property changes the text color?
questions.explanation: The CSS color property controls text color.

questions.question: Which HTML element loads a JavaScript file?
questions.explanation: The script element can load or contain JavaScript.

questions.question: What does DOM stand for?
questions.explanation: The DOM is the browser's object model for a document.

questions.question: Which method is commonly used to fetch JSON from a URL in modern JavaScript?
questions.explanation: fetch() returns a Promise for a network response.

questions.question: Which CSS layout module is designed for one-dimensional layouts?
questions.explanation: Flexbox is designed around one main axis.

questions.question: What does a 404 HTTP status usually mean?
questions.explanation: 404 means the requested resource was not found.

questions.question: Which attribute gives an image alternative text?
questions.explanation: The alt attribute describes an image for assistive technologies and fallback display.

questions.question: What is localStorage used for?
answers: Storing small key-value data in the browser
questions.explanation: localStorage stores strings in the browser without an expiration date.

questions.question: Which JavaScript keyword declares a block-scoped variable that can be reassigned?
questions.explanation: let creates a block-scoped binding whose value can be reassigned.
